## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, I've got a great case study for you. This is a quick launch case study with Wolf Elkenner. And so the great thing about quick launches is that you can bring in a lot of money with very, very little effort. It is for people that already have a business, you already have a list, you already have an offer. And basically what you're putting together is you're going to put together a special offer, call it a sale. I don't like to put things on sale. So I go to great lengths to avoid having sales because I think in general, when you start putting stuff on sale regularly, then all you've done is you've reduced your prices. And in the online environment, it's basically you're always in a battle of maintaining your perceived value. If you're selling training products, it's hard for people to judge the value of your products. So you have to tell them what the value is. So you go to great lengths to establish what the value is. And then if all of a sudden you start discounting your products, then it establishes a new value. It hurts your positioning. And basically people are only gonna buy from you. You don't realize they have that sale on every single week. When you have an email list, they're gonna be watching your emails and if you put something on sale every single week, they're gonna know it. So in general, I like to avoid having sales, but the quick launch is basically a way to have a sale because you introduce a reason why and you tell a story behind that reason why. And it's not something you wanna do often, but whenever you do need a quick surge of sales, it's really cool. So you're gonna see how Will did it here and he did a really nice job with it. for $45, wasn't hardly selling at all. And then he added in a special report, a one hour Q&A webinar, not too hard to do, and he dropped the price to $25. And he did this as a four day sale. Now, another key thing here is if you've studied my work, especially like in launching your list, the launching your list bonus course you get, I said, it's been my experience over the years. You know, there's two primary types of lists. There's a list of prospects, people that haven't bought from you yet and a list of clients or customers who have bought from you. In other words, the next time you go to do a promotion for every subscriber that's on your client list and you compare that to a subscriber on your prospect list, your client list is gonna generate 15 times greater results. ## [15:08 - 16:32]

to close on Memorial Day. So he extended it one day and he gave us reason why. You can totally do this. You can totally do this. And, and this is the day. When you do, when you have this, what they call it reason why copy, which is you tell people why you're doing something.
